title: Panicum fragile
taxonomy: Panicum fragile is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is further classified within the genus Panicum, which consists of various species of grasses. Panicum fragile is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Old World, specifically in Australia, Asia, and Africa. Within the genus Panicum, P. fragile is often placed in the subgenus Panicoideae, which includes many species of grasses. The specific classification of P. fragile is still subject to ongoing research and revisions in the field of botany.

intro: Panicum fragile, also known as Fragile Panicgrass, is a perennial grass native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its delicate stems and small, pale yellow flowers. Traditionally, Fragile Panicgrass is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial in treating various health issues, such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Panicum fragile include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional Asian medicine, Fragile Panicgrass is commonly prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
